data.body: PASS-1 review (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety [overlay]): PASS not-applicable, no findings. This plan ships git-tracked flat-file artifacts only — Inspect-AI eval YAML specs (`.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` / packaged `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml`), prompt front-matter (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`), and labeled eval cases. There is no schema change, no persisted-format change, no DDL, and no backfill of a deployed/persisted data store. The "auto-labels seed the corpus" / "gating gold subset" language describes authoring labeled eval-case data into git-tracked files, not migrating a live store. T11's overlay precondition (changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data) is not met; its own ANTI-FP rule directs not-applicable for non-persisted changes — raising migration-execution findings here would itself be a false-fire of the class this ticket aims to suppress.
